LAST UPDATED: —  ·  521 STORIES
📋 SITUATION BRIEF ▶
Hundreds killed in Haiti drone strikes, including 60 civilians, Human Rights Watch says Reuters Greatly weakened during the 2024 war, Hezbollah has faced pressure from the Lebanese state to disarm. Kevin Warsh's path to leading the Federal Reserve faces a hurdle as a Senate member holds up the nomination over ongoing Justice Department probe concerns. On energy: Chris Wright said it will ensure that oil remains flowing to global markets In financial news: The Wall Street Journal reported brands are now marketing products specifically to children as young as 3 years old. Politically: In the first year of President Donald Trump's second term, ICE Houston arrested more than 400 illegal aliens charged or convicted for child sex offenses. Streets in the commercial district lie strewn with debris after the Uba River overflows, collapsing a bridge and several buildings, in Minas Gerais State, Brazil. The officer was reportedly transported to Shock Trauma for treatment, while the suspect was also shot. Researchers say elevated p-tau217 levels in older women were strongly linked to future dementia, raising hopes for earlier detection and prevention strategies. Former DC police officer accused of sexually assaulting 10 women he met on dating apps, in person.
📅 YESTERDAY — Monday, March 9 (115 stories) ▶
Europe rallies around Cyprus days after the Iran war’s first drone attack on EU territory AP News Heaviest day of strikes yet on Iran despite market bets that war will end soon Reuters MK Ariel Kallner to Arutz Sheva: Hezbollah must be dealt with just like Iran Israel National News On energy: Oil prices sink 13% as Trump predicts Middle East de-escalation Reuters Politically: Trump pushes GOP on voting bill, demanding an end to most mail balloting AP News Shang Dynasty oracle bones and modern weather models feature in the same study. German publishers reject Apple's revised app tracking rules, urge antitrust fine Reuters China’s exports turbocharge into 2026 after record-breaking year Reuters

Judge blocks Perplexity’s AI agents from shopping on Amazon
The Verge  ·  2:11 PM
L
▼
R
A federal judge has issued an order blocking Perplexity's web browser-based AI agents from placing Amazon orders on a user's behalf, as reported earlier by Bloomberg. In a ruling on Monday, US District Judge Maxine Chesney writes that Amazon has "provided strong evidence" that Perplexity's Comet browser accesses user accounts "without authorization" from the retail giant. Amazon sued Perplexity in November, alleging that it "repeatedly requested" that the AI startup stop letting its agents buy products for customers.

Satechi’s new folding dock adds USB, audio, and video ports to the iPad
The Verge  ·  12:00 PM
L
▼
R
Satechi has announced a redesigned version of the iPad Pro stand with USB-C hub functionality it launched nearly five years ago that's smaller and easier to travel with. The new OntheGo Foldable Stand Hub is available now through Satechi's online store and Amazon for $79.99 in space black. While the original was specifically designed for iPad Pro models, Satechi says the new stand hub will work with all iPads released after 2021, as well as other USB-C mobile devices and laptops.

Nvidia’s DLSS 4.5 with 6x Frame Generation is rolling out at the end of March
The Verge  ·  11:30 AM
L
▼
R
Nvidia's DLSS 4.5 with 6x Multi Frame Generation will be available starting March 31st for users with RTX 50-series GPUs, the company announced on Tuesday. With 6x Multi Frame Generation, Nvidia claims that DLSS 4.5 can generate "five additional frames for every single natively rendered one, for a maximum 6X multiplier." It's an increase from the maximum of three additional frames possible with DLSS 4. On March 31st, Nvidia will also release Dynamic Frame Generation for 50-series GPUs, which can automatically switch between Multi Frame Generation multipliers to hit your target frame rate for a game or your display's refresh rate.
Meta acquires Moltbook, the Reddit-like network for AI agents
The Verge  ·  11:22 AM
L
▼
R
Meta is acquiring Moltbook, a Reddit-like platform where AI agents can make and comment on posts, as first reported by Axios. In a statement to The Verge, Meta spokesperson Matthew Tye confirmed the Moltbook team will join Meta Superintelligence Labs as the company looks for "new ways for AI agents to work for people and businesses." Matt Schlicht and Ben Parr launched Moltbook earlier this year, offering a "social" network for autonomous agents powered by the open-source AI assistant OpenClaw (formerly Moltbot). The platform went viral earlier this year for a number of posts - including one that asks questions about AI consciousness - thou … Read the full story at The Verge.
